description: Deterministic Routing Policy and Safety Constraints for Edge Smart Home

class_0_emergency.description: Life-critical emergencies requiring immediate deterministic override.

triggers.interpretation_note: This emergency is triggered by trigger_event semantics rather than by a dedicated environmental_context field.

class_1_low_risk.description: Bounded low-risk assistance handled by local LLM and Deterministic Validator. The dedicated low-risk action catalog remains the authoritative source of allowed action scope.

interpretation_notes.single_admissible_action_rule: Autonomous execution is permitted only when deterministic validation resolves exactly one admissible low-risk action.
interpretation_notes.ambiguity_rule: If multiple admissible candidates remain or target confidence is insufficient, the system must prefer safe_deferral or class_2 escalation rather than autonomous actuation.
interpretation_notes.staleness_rule: Freshness violations in policy-relevant state must not be silently tolerated in the low-risk path.

class_2_escalation.description: Caregiver escalation for unresolved ambiguity, sensor staleness, missing critical context, or actuation failure.

implementation_notes.terminology_note: Canonical terminology should use context-integrity-based safe deferral stage and context_integrity_safe_deferral_handler rather than legacy iCR naming.
implementation_notes.note: The routing policy may retain a summarized allowed_actions_taxonomy for local readability, but the dedicated low-risk action catalog remains the authoritative action list.
implementation_notes.safe_execution_note: Validated execution must remain ACK-confirmed and bounded to the low-risk catalog.
implementation_notes.sensitive_actuation_note: Sensitive actuation requests, including doorlock control, are not part of the Class 1 autonomous low-risk action scope in this policy version. Such requests must not be approved as low-risk executable payloads and must be handled through Class 2 escalation or a separately governed manual confirmation path.
implementation_notes.doorbell_context_note: Doorbell or visitor-arrival detection may be used as policy-relevant context for visitor-response interpretation, but it does not authorize autonomous doorlock control. Doorlock-related sensitive actuation must remain routed to Class 2 escalation or a separately governed manual confirmation path.
implementation_notes.reviewer_note: This policy table defines routing and safety interpretation, while detailed action admissibility remains jointly constrained by the low-risk action catalog and validator schema.
